behaviors.description: Products marked as out-of-stock are excluded from the recommendation results regardless of their relevance score.
behaviors.justification: Mocking product data hides failures where filtering logic never actually consults the stock field. Real product data structures and actual filtering implementation are needed to verify the stock status is properly evaluated. The failure boundary is in data-layer integration—whether the code reads the real stock field.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that loads products with stock=false into a test database, requests recommendations, and verifies those products are excluded from results even if their relevance score is high.

behaviors.description: The user preference filter correctly excludes product categories the user has explicitly blocked in their settings.
behaviors.justification: Mocked user preferences always return expected values, hiding failures in whether blocked_categories field is actually read. Real user settings data and actual filtering logic must integrate correctly. Unit mocks could pass even if the code ignores user preferences entirely.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that sets up a user with specific blocked categories in preferences, requests recommendations, and verifies no products from those categories appear in results.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm ranks products that match the user's recent browsing patterns higher than products with no browsing signal.
behaviors.justification: Mocking the ranking algorithm could return pre-ranked results without actually weighting browsing patterns. Real browsing history and actual algorithm logic are needed to verify browsing signals genuinely influence rankings. The failure is in algorithm+data integration, not just logic.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that records specific browsing history for a user (products/categories visited), requests recommendations, and verifies products matching that history rank higher than products with no browsing signal.

behaviors.description: Pagination returns the correct page of results, with stable ordering — requesting page 2 twice returns identical results if the underlying data hasn't changed.
behaviors.justification: Mocked pagination could always return consistent results even if the real ordering logic is non-deterministic (e.g., iterates over unordered sets). Only real data and multiple actual requests reveal ordering instability. The failure boundary is whether pagination produces deterministic ordering across invocations.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that requests page 2 of recommendations twice without intervening state changes, and verifies both responses return identical products in identical order.

behaviors.description: The recommendation algorithm surfaces at least one product from a category the user hasn't browsed before (serendipity requirement), avoiding a filter bubble.
behaviors.justification: Mocked algorithm could hardcode one product from a new category without applying real diversity logic. Real product catalog with multiple categories and actual algorithm logic are needed to verify serendipity weighting is implemented. Mocks hide whether diversity is deliberate or accidental.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that creates a user with browsing history limited to categories A and B, requests recommendations, and verifies at least one product from a different category appears in results.

behaviors.description: A new user with no purchase or browsing history receives recommendations based on overall popularity rather than an empty result set.
behaviors.justification: Mocked algorithm and catalog could return results even if real code crashes or throws an exception on empty history. Real product data with popularity scores and actual fallback logic are needed to verify graceful degradation. The failure is at a critical boundary condition.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that creates a new user with empty purchase and browsing history, requests recommendations, and verifies a non-empty result set of popular products is returned.

behaviors.description: Click tracking records the correct product ID, position in the list, and timestamp for each recommendation the user interacts with.
behaviors.justification: Mocking the tracking service verifies the call happens but misses data transformation errors—off-by-one in position calculation, timezone bugs in timestamp, stale product references. Real click events and actual tracking infrastructure reveal whether data is recorded correctly.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that simulates user clicking on a specific recommendation, verifies the click event is recorded in the tracking system with exact product ID, correct position (accounting for pagination offset), and valid timestamp.

behaviors.description: The response time for generating recommendations stays under 200ms for a catalog of 100,000 products.
behaviors.justification: Integration tests with fast local databases or in-memory data can hide real performance failures that only emerge with 100k products and realistic database I/O. Performance is inherently a system-level property—failures only manifest under realistic scale, network conditions, and hardware constraints.
behaviors.test_description: System test that loads 100,000 realistic products into a database with production-like I/O characteristics, requests recommendations, and measures end-to-end response time to verify it stays consistently under 200ms under realistic conditions.

behaviors.description: When a recommended product is purchased by the user, the recommendation algorithm deprioritizes it in subsequent requests.
behaviors.justification: Mocked ranking algorithm could ignore purchase state and still return ranked results. Real purchase history stored in the system and actual algorithm logic across multiple requests are needed to verify purchased products genuinely change rank. The failure is in state-dependent ranking.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that requests recommendations for a user, marks a recommended product as purchased, requests recommendations again, and verifies the purchased product ranks lower (appears later or disappears) in the new results.

behaviors.description: The recommendation results are deterministic for the same user state — requesting recommendations twice with no intervening user activity returns the same ranked list.
behaviors.justification: Mocked algorithm always returns consistent results even if real algorithm uses randomness (shuffled results, non-deterministic set iteration, or random tie-breaking). Multiple invocations of the actual algorithm are needed to detect non-determinism that violates the requirement.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test that requests recommendations for a user twice with identical state and no intervening activity, and verifies both responses return identical products in identical order (full result list match).
